mechanism_narrative: A low or falling FEDFUNDS rate compresses discount rates and supports equity valuation multiples, so S&P 500 forward returns over the next month are positive when policy remains near zero or declines further.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward returns are not above the unconditional median when FEDFUNDS is flat or falling, or if triggered windows produce negative average returns over any 2-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent trend indicates labor-market deterioration and weaker household income expectations, which depresses equity prices over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if episodes with a 0.3 percentage-point 3-month rise in UNRATE do not show lower-than-baseline 20-day S&P 500 returns, or if the sign is positive in a majority of such episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A broad expansion in RSAFS alongside rising PAYEMS signals stronger nominal spending and payroll income, which lifts expected revenues and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined positive-slope signal in RSAFS and PAYEMS does not outperform either series alone, or if 20-day forward returns are not positive more than 55% of the time when both rise together.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in INDPRO indicates stronger industrial output and cyclical production momentum, which transmits to earnings expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over a one-quarter horizon.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward S&P 500 returns are not higher after 2-month INDPRO gains above 1%, or if the effect disappears in more than two consecutive rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained downturn in HOUST reflects weaker housing activity, lower cyclical demand, and softer risk appetite, which should translate into negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if large HOUST declines are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns more often than not, or if average forward return is not below zero in the triggered sample.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains pinned near zero, real rates fall and liquidity conditions improve, supporting equity multiples and positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the inflation-with-low-rates regime does not produce above-baseline 20-day returns, or if the signal turns negative when CPIAUCSL is above 2.5% but FEDFUNDS stays below 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: In a labor-market recovery phase, a decline in UNRATE confirms easing recession risk and improving household cash flow, which should generate positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Reject if falling UNRATE paired with rising PAYEMS fails to predict higher 60-day forward returns, or if the signal is weaker than unconditional returns in recovery windows.

mechanism_narrative: During a late-cycle slowdown, soft RSAFS combined with weak GDPC1 growth indicates deteriorating final demand and earnings revisions, which should pressure the S&P 500 lower over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if weak RSAFS and weak GDPC1 do not coincide with negative 60-day forward returns, or if the combined signal has no explanatory gain over either series alone.
